deployment.name: EIE Cost Estimation (reproduction)
deployment.description: Reproduction. Maps the EIE team's 'normal cycle' measurement (84,490 input / 70,656 cached / 854 output across 6 stages → $0.05/cycle) into the calc's agent model. Their headline math: 400 DAU × 50 cycles/user/day × 30 days × $0.05/cycle = $30K/mo unoptimized — equivalent to MAU=10K, sessions/day=0.2, questions/session=10, no bot factor (10K × 0.2 × 10 × 30 = 600K cycles/mo). This preset hits the same ~$30K headline (mix=worst, applyBotFactor=false, verification/FedRAMP/infra all off) so a procurement reviewer can pull on the same levers the paper exposes — switch mix to mixed (~$18K), enable FedRAMP (+15% on LLM line), turn on verification, or flip the tool-response architecture to see where the ~3-6× key-fields lever lands. The agent.input_tokens = 14,062 sits between the public-geospatial-qa preset's templated anchor (2,933) and freeform anchor (23,740) — closer to a realistic key-fields midpoint, ~4.8× the templated floor.

anchor_query.example: Methane datasets over Alabama 2022 — dataset query → selection → location+year filter → datetime confirmation → location confirmation → run analysis (6 stages).

_calibration.source: EIE Cost Estimation document. Normal-cycle measurement on GPT-5.2.
_calibration.method: Per-stage average from EIE's table: 84,490 input / 6 stages = 14,082 input/turn; 70,656 cached / 84,490 input = 0.836 cache rate; 854 output / 6 stages = 142 output/turn. Per-call cost = 2,310 fresh × $1.75/M + 11,772 cached × $0.175/M + 142 × $14/M = $0.00809. Per-cycle (6 calls) ≈ $0.0485, EIE rounds to $0.05.
_calibration.supersedes: Lower-anchor 'public-geospatial-qa' preset measured per-turn input at 2,933 (templated) / 23,740 (freeform). EIE's 14,082/turn sits ~4.8× the templated floor — between the paper's reproduced 'key-fields midpoint' (~3×) and full freeform (~8×). Useful for reasoning about EIE's $30K/mo claim against the paper's $1.5K/mo templated baseline.
_calibration.to_reproduce_long_cycle: Set calls_per_query=10 (instead of 6), input_tokens≈12930, cache≈0.887 → per-cycle ≈ $0.065. EIE's optimized variants reduce input slightly and cut output ~40% via tighter prompting and better state management.
_calibration.to_reproduce_eie_traffic_math: DAU=400, cycles/user/day=50 → 20K cycles/day → 600K cycles/mo. Paper formula at MAU=10K, sessions/day=0.2, questions/session=10, no bot factor: 10K × 0.2 × 10 × 30 × 1.0 = 600K queries/mo. The two framings give the same total. EIE's headline = 600K × $0.05 = $30K.
_calibration.gap_to_paper: Per-cycle cost ($0.05) is ~30× the paper's templated anchor ($0.00168) and ~3× the paper's freeform anchor ($0.0147). That gap is the tool-response architecture lever in action — adopting EIE's measured 14K tokens/turn versus the paper's status-only-style 3K tokens/turn explains the bulk of the difference. Bot factor and shape mix are second-order vs. that.

set_datetime.label: parse_datetime
set_datetime.description: Validate and normalize a date range (Stage 3 / Stage 4 of the EIE cycle). Templated returns the parsed interval + confirmation prompt.

get_place.label: geocode
get_place.description: Resolve a place name to bbox + admin geometry (Stage 3 / Stage 5 of the EIE cycle). Templated returns confirmation; freeform returns the full GeoJSON polygon.

collections_rag.label: search_collections
collections_rag.description: Semantic search over the STAC catalog (Stage 1 of the EIE cycle — 'I want methane datasets'). Templated returns just the count + selection prompt; freeform returns the Top-K collection metadata.

select_collection.label: select_collection
select_collection.description: Records the user's collection + variable selection (Stage 2 of the EIE cycle — 'ct-ch4-monthgrid-v2025'). Small either way.

stac_search.label: search_items
stac_search.description: Enumerate catalog items inside the AOI and datetime window (Stage 6 of the EIE cycle — 'run analysis on items'). Templated returns just the count; freeform returns the full STAC item array.

stats.label: compute_stats
stats.description: Per-item zonal statistics (Stage 6 of the EIE cycle). Templated returns just the count; freeform returns the per-item stats dictionaries.

viz.label: render_map
viz.description: Build a raster tile URL for the selected items + AOI (Stage 6 of the EIE cycle). Small either way — visualization metadata is bounded.

agents.label: EIE Agent (NASA-IMPACT, 6-stage cycle)
agents.description: EIE-agent's measured per-cycle behavior. Per call: 14,062 fresh conversation-history input + 142 output at 0.836 cache rate, GPT-5.2 pricing. Tool returns add once per query (sum 119 tok templated, 20,926 tok freeform). Per query: 6 × 14,062 + 119 = 84,491 ≈ EIE's measured 84,490 input/cycle. Per cycle ≈ $0.0485 ≈ EIE's stated $0.05. Switch tool_response_mode to freeform (or flip individual tools' RETURN SHAPE) to see the heavy-payload variant — EIE's 'normal cycle' was measured under their typical templated/key-fields tool returns. To model the 'long with clarifications' scenario, raise calls_per_query to 10.

worst.label: Worst-case (every cycle full pipeline · matches EIE doc)

mixed.label: Mixed (40% full · 30% rag · 15% partial · 10% refusal · 5% heavy)

segments.label: EIE active users
segments.description: EIE projection: ~10K MAU baseline, ~400 DAU. Paper formula 10K × 0.2 × 10 × 30 × 1.0 = 600K cycles/mo matches EIE's 400 DAU × 50 cycles/day × 30. applyBotFactor=false because EIE's '50 cycles/user/day' is an aggregate that already includes any bot or scripted traffic — adding a 1.5× bot multiplier on top would double-count.
